title: Hieracium psammogenes
intro: Hieracium psammogenes, also known as sand hawkweed or rock hawkweed, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in wound healing and reduce swelling. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium psammogenes include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Hieracium psammogenes include tinctures, infusions, and salves, which are often used to treat minor wounds, skin irritations, and infections.
taxonomy: Hieracium psammogenes is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family (Asteraceae). This plant is commonly known as a hawkweed. It belongs to the genus Hieracium, which comprises over 1000 species. The classification of Hieracium psammogenes places it within the subfamily Cichorioideae and tribe Cichorieae. Further taxonomic classification may be necessary for a more detailed understanding of its relationships within the plant kingdom.
